Run dependency installation, builds, and sample downloads top-to-bottom, left-to-right. Click Install/Build on each card to run in background.
Installs system packages for C++ build (cmake, gcc, ninja, OpenCV). Click Install → check real-time progress in log area. Shows ✅ badge when done.
Compiles C++ demo binaries with cmake Release mode. Run after step ① is complete. Build takes about 2-5 minutes.
Downloads pre-compiled sample models (.dxnn) and demo videos for Run Inference and Benchmark.
Installs packages required for NPU runtime (cmake, ONNX Runtime, etc.).
Installs the DeepX NPU hardware driver. Requires sudo password — click ⌨️ Manual Input below to open the stdin field.
Installs the ONNX→DXNN compiler. Requires a DEEPX Developers Portal account. A credential form will appear.
Real-time logs for each install/build task appear here. Use 🔄 Refresh Status to update all, and ⌨️ Manual Input for sudo passwords.
Click ▶ Run Diagnostics to auto-check system after installation. Verifies NPU driver, runtime version, and dependencies to detect issues early.
Enter DeepX Developers Portal credentials (username, password) for DX-COM compiler installation. Sign up on the Portal if you don't have an account.
View and filter installed AI models
Download sample models in Setup first.
Filter models by Detection, Classification, Segmentation, Pose, Depth etc. Click a chip to show only that category.
Type a model name for real-time table filtering. e.g., "yolo" shows YOLOv5, YOLOv8, etc.
View each model's name, category, C++/Python support, mode (Sync/Async), meta info, file size. Use Actions column for quick run.
Run AI inference on a single image or video
📷 Single: Inference on one image/video. 🔄 Continuous: Continuous inference on streams. Click tabs to switch.
🖼 Image: Single image inference. 🎬 Video: Per-frame video inference. Switching radio buttons changes the input area below.

Select AI task category: Detection, Classification, Segmentation, Pose etc. Model list updates accordingly. Selecting a category shows task-specific parameters (Classification → Top-K, Segmentation → Alpha, etc.).
Select the AI model from chosen category. e.g., YOLOv8n, ResNet50, DeepLabV3.
Choose C++ (compiled, high-performance) or Python (scripted). C++ is generally faster.
Sync: Process next frame after current completes. Async: Pipeline inference and preprocessing for higher FPS.
Select the NPU device number for inference. On multi-NPU systems, you can target a specific device.
Minimum confidence threshold (0-1) for model output. Higher = fewer but more confident results. Default 0.25 for Detection.
Non-Maximum Suppression IoU threshold. Controls overlapping bounding box removal. Lower = more aggressive dedup. Detection only.
Slider shown when Classification category is selected. Displays the top K classification results. Higher values include more candidate classes (default: 5).
Slider shown when Segmentation category is selected. Adjusts mask overlay transparency (0-1). 0 = transparent, 1 = opaque (default: 0.6).
Click a sample image thumbnail to use as input. In video mode, a video file dropdown appears.
After configuration, click to run AI inference on NPU. Results (bounding boxes, classification, etc.) appear in the Result area.
After inference, shows result image, FPS, latency, detected object info. Click image for full-size preview.
When checked, performance profiling runs alongside inference. A Gantt chart below shows timing for each stage (preprocessing, NPU inference, post-processing).
Package source code, config, and model file of current model for download on Outputs page. Choose C++/Python/Both.
Select the language for the export package: Both (C++ & Python), C++ only, or Python only.
Click 📦 Export to package model source code, config, and binary in the selected language. Download from the Outputs page.
Click to immediately stop the running inference. Useful for stopping long video inference midway.
Dropdown that appears when 🎬 Video input type is selected. Choose from the list of downloaded sample video files.
Select input source: 🎬 Video (file), 📹 Camera (USB webcam), 📡 RTSP (network stream).
Select C++/Python language and Sync/Async mode. Async pipelines preprocessing and inference for higher FPS.
Select NPU device for continuous inference. All slots run on the same device.
Add up to 8 model slots to run different AI models simultaneously. Select category and model in each slot.
Click ＋ Add Model to add a new slot. Up to 8 slots, each independently configurable.
Click to start continuous inference. View MJPEG live streams for each slot in the Live Display on the right.
Shows elapsed time when continuous inference is running. Blinking red dot = inference active.
Inference results for each slot shown in a grid. In video/camera mode, view MJPEG live streams for real-time results.
After stopping, shows average FPS, total frames, processing time summary for each slot.
Click to stop inference on all slots immediately. Performance summary is shown after stopping.
Appears when 📹 Camera input is selected. Choose from connected USB webcam devices (/dev/video0, etc.).
Appears when 📡 RTSP input is selected. Enter the RTSP server address (IP:Port) of the network camera.
Set category, language, mode, input type in the left panel. Set Loop Count below for iterations. Higher = more accurate FPS.
Select AI task category for benchmarking. The model table on the right filters to show only matching models.
Select benchmark input: 🖼 Image, 🎬 Video, 📹 Camera, 📡 RTSP. Image mode repeats Loop Count times; Video mode processes that many frames.
Number of inference iterations. Default 100 = run 100 inferences per model and average FPS. For video, processes that many frames.
Select models to benchmark via checkboxes in the right table. ☑ All to select all, ☐ None to deselect.
Runs benchmark sequentially on selected models. Each model runs specified iterations and measures FPS/Latency.
After benchmarking, a results table (per-model FPS, Latency, Status) and FPS comparison chart appear below. Use 📄 Export Report to download.
Visualizes benchmark results as a bar chart. Compare FPS across models at a glance to find the optimal one.
Click to stop the current model's benchmark. Results for already completed models are preserved.
Select slot count (2/4/6/8) at top. More slots = more simultaneous model comparisons.
Select input type: File, Camera, RTSP. All slots share the same input for fair comparison.
Select category and model independently in each slot. e.g., Slot A = YOLOv5s, Slot B = YOLOv8n for comparison.
Click ▶ Run All to run all slots simultaneously. Each slot displays its result image and FPS/Latency for visual comparison.
After all slots finish, a Performance Comparison table appears. Compare model name, FPS, Latency per slot to find the optimal model.
🔒 Internal: Private model repository. 🌐 Public: Public model repo. Select based on network environment.
Filter models by task type: Detection, Classification, Segmentation, Pose, etc.
View each model's task, name, class, dataset, input size, params, license, accuracy. Download Q-Lite/Q-Pro DXNN and JSON config individually.
Check model checkboxes to show the cart bar at bottom. Use ☑ Select All, 🆕 New Only, Q-Lite All, Q-Pro All for quick selection, then batch download from cart.
Compile ONNX models to DXNN for NPU
Shows DX-COM compiler install status and version. ✅ = available, ⏳ = checking, ❌ = not installed.
One-click auto-fill compile settings for common models. Click ResNet50, YOLOv5s, YOLOv8n, etc. to auto-populate ONNX path, preprocessing, PPU.
Drag & drop or click to upload an ONNX file. Or enter server path below and click 🔍 Inspect to analyze structure.
Upload a previously exported compile config JSON file to auto-restore all settings: preprocessing, PPU, input shape, etc.
Specify calibration dataset path for quantization. Select a folder with representative input images. Critical for maintaining accuracy.
Specify the output directory path for compiled DXNN model files. Leave empty for default path (compiler_output/).
Select the DXQ Enhanced quantization scheme. Configure quantization method for optimal balance between model accuracy and performance.
Select the device (CPU or CUDA) for quantization calibration. CUDA GPU significantly speeds up calibration.
Configure the preprocessing pipeline (resize, normalize, convertColor, etc.). Select type from dropdown and ＋ Add. Order matters.
For Object Detection, enable PPU to run NMS post-processing on NPU. Choose Type 0 (Anchor: YOLOv5/v7) or Type 1 (Anchor-Free: YOLOv8/YOLOX).
Specify model input tensor shape. N=batch, C=channels(RGB=3), H=height, W=width. e.g., 1,3,640,640. Auto-detected via Inspect, but can be manually adjusted.
Set calibration method (EMA: exponential moving average, MinMax) and sample count. EMA generally yields better quantization. Optimization Level controls optimization intensity.
Click to start ONNX→DXNN compilation. Progress bar and real-time logs shown. 📋 Export JSON exports config as JSON.
Real-time compile logs appear here. Errors shown in red. Progress bar tracks compilation.

4 visualization modes:
• ONNX Graph — View original ONNX layers in DX-TRON
• DXNN Graph — View optimized compiled .dxnn graph
• ⚡ Side-by-Side — Compare ONNX (Netron) and DXNN (DX-TRON) side by side
• Netron ↗ — Open external Netron viewer in new tab

After compilation, a result summary card appears. Use View DXNN Graph to inspect, Test Run for quick inference, Deploy to add to Models page. Compiled models can be directly benchmarked against other models on the Benchmark page.
View history of previous compilations (model, status, duration, category). From each entry, run Test Run or Deploy.
Click to immediately stop the current ONNX→DXNN compilation. Use to cancel long-running compilations.
View and download inference output files
Switch between 📊 Table and 🖼 Grid views in the toolbar. Grid shows thumbnails; Table shows detailed file listings.
Filter by file type: images, videos, archives (ZIP). Quickly find specific output file types.
In Grid view, output files appear as thumbnail cards. Click a card for Lightbox preview. Each card has a 📥 download button.
View all output files (images, videos, reports, packages) from Run Inference, Benchmark, Compare, Export. Shows size, modified time, with download buttons.
Click an image/video to open full-screen Lightbox. Use 📥 Download and Before/After Compare buttons to overlay-compare original and result.
Ask AI chatbot about models, SDK, and compilation
Click the 💬 button at bottom-right to open the DX Assistant chatbot window.
Suggested question buttons for common queries. Click to auto-input and get immediate answers about the DeepX platform.
Type your question freely in the input field and click Send. Answers questions about models, SDK, compiler, and hardware.
Choose an AI provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, local, etc.) in chat settings (⚙️), then type questions below. Suggested questions provided. Answers about DeepX models, SDK, compiler, etc.
Sidebar, top bar, notifications, NPU monitor
Navigate to all pages from the left sidebar. Use ☰ button to collapse/expand.
Contains page title, language toggle (EN/KO), NPU temp, memory bar, and 🎓 Tutorial buttons.
Click EN/KO to switch English↔Korean. Setting saved in localStorage, persists after refresh.
Notification drawer panel opened by clicking the 🔔 bell icon. View notification history including system alerts, installation completions, and errors.
Action results appear as toast notifications at bottom-right. Green=success, Red=error, Yellow=warning. Auto-dismiss after 3s.
Inference performance profiling and Gantt chart analysis
Check the 📊 Profiler checkbox on Run Inference page to collect performance profiling data during inference.
After inference, profiling results appear here. Visually analyze the time spent in each processing stage.
Gantt chart shows each inference pipeline stage (preprocessing, NPU inference, post-processing) on a timeline. Bar length = stage duration. Zoom/pan with mouse.
See which processing stage each color represents. Typically: preprocessing (blue), NPU inference (green), post-processing (orange).
Shows average time, min/max, total pipeline latency per stage. Use to identify bottlenecks and determine optimization direction.
Auto-installs C++ build deps. Check logs after Install.
Compiles C++ binaries. Run after dependencies.
Downloads pre-compiled models and demo videos.
Run diagnostics to check installation status.
Install packages required for NPU runtime (cmake, ONNX Runtime, etc.).
Install DEEPX NPU kernel driver (requires sudo).
ONNX→.dxnn converter. Requires DEEPX Developers Portal account.
Real-time logs. Use stdin for sudo passwords.
DEEPX developer portal account required for DX-COM downloads.
Used only by setup steps that require portal authentication.
Name, category, C++/Python, mode, file size. Quick run via Actions.
Select the model task category for Single inference.
Choose the model to run from the selected category.
Select the Single inference runtime language, C++ or Python.
Sync: sequential. Async: pipelined for higher FPS.
Select NPU device number. For multi-NPU environments.
Min confidence threshold (0-1). Higher = more confident.
Overlap box removal threshold. Detection only.
Show top K results for Classification.
When enabled, runs profiling alongside inference.
Run inference for the selected input with the current Single settings.
Stop the running single inference job.
Area to pick images for Single inference.
Choose image input mode for Single inference.
Pick sample images or compare with uploaded inputs.
Result image, FPS, Latency. Click image to enlarge.
Shows the pre-run guidance and detailed results after inference.
Package model source+config+binary. Download from Outputs.
Select which example-code languages to include in the export.
Create a runnable package from the current model and settings.
Select the input source for continuous inference.
Select how Continuous inference consumes input streams.
Select the NPU device for continuous inference.
Set number of parallel inference slots.
Add model to a slot with current settings.
Stop the running Continuous inference session.
Shows elapsed time for the continuous inference run.
Review per-slot FPS, throughput, and status summary.
Displays real-time inference results for active slots.
Set category, language, mode, input, loop count.
Check models to benchmark. All/None buttons.
Sequential benchmark on selected models. Measures FPS/Latency.
Set number of inference repetitions per model.
Summary card shown after benchmark completes.
Choose 2/4/6/8 slots. All share same input.
Select models individually per slot for simultaneous comparison.
Run all slots simultaneously and compare results.
Download Q-Lite/Q-Pro DXNN+JSON individually. Cart via checkboxes.
Cart for batch downloading selected models.
Drag & drop or enter server path. Inspect to analyze structure.
Load previous config JSON to auto-restore.
Specify calibration dataset path for quantization.
Configure resize, normalize etc. pipeline. Order matters.
Run Detection post-processing on NPU. Type 0/1.
N,C,H,W tensor shape. Auto-detected via Inspect.
EMA/MinMax method, sample count, optimization level.
Previous compile history. Test Run/Deploy available.
Start DXNN compilation with the current ONNX, config, and calibration settings.
Shows whether the compiler is installed and available.
Path for generated DXNN, logs, and intermediate files.
Choose a DXQ enhanced quantization scheme.
Select the device used during calibration and quantization.
Inspect live compile progress and error messages.
Grid/Table view toggle. File type filters.
Thumbnail cards in Grid view. Click for Lightbox.
Shown when there are no output files yet.
Shows image and inference results in detail.
Compare original and result with a Before/After slider.
Timeline for each inference pipeline stage. Zoom/pan.
Sidebar bottom → model add/delete, skeleton, Git, package extraction.
Main header of the Reference page. Starting point for document navigation.
Browse detailed DX App guides, parameters, and workflows in one view.
Type keywords to instantly filter matching reference cards.
Use chips to show only topics such as Setup, Run, Benchmark, or Compiler.
Descriptions, tips, and workflow cards for the selected topics appear here.
Jump to a group of related DX App reference topics.
Open a focused guide for this feature, parameter group, or workflow.
Control to open and close the detail view area.
Detail area showing full content, tips, and examples for the selected topic.
Open the AI assistant while using DX App.
Shows conversation history, suggestions, and responses.
Type a question and send it with Enter or the send button.
Question chips for quickly starting common tasks.

6-step setup:

  • ① DX-APP Dependencies
  • ② DX-APP Build
  • ③ Sample Assets
  • ④ DX-Runtime Deps
  • ⑤ NPU Driver (sudo)
  • ⑥ DX-COM Compiler (Portal account)

• Single — one image/video
  • Continuous — video/camera/RTSP, up to 8 slots
  • Params: Confidence, NMS, Top-K, Alpha
  • ROI selection
  • Export Package

• Multi-model selection → sequential benchmark
  • Loop count setting
  • FPS chart + results table
  • 📄 Export Report

• 2-8 slot simultaneous comparison
  • Shared input
  • Performance comparison table

• Internal / Public sources
  • Task filter + search
  • Q-Lite / Q-Pro DXNN download
  • Cart batch download

• Upload → Inspect → Preprocessing → PPU → Compile → Test → Deploy
  • Presets for one-click setup
  • JSON config load/export
  • Graph visualization
  • Compile history

• Esc — Close tutorial / TOC
  • ←/→ — Tutorial prev/next
  • Enter — Next step

• Enable Profiler checkbox in Run Inference
  • Gantt chart shows pipeline timeline after inference
  • Per-stage timing: preprocessing/NPU inference/post-processing
  • Zoom/pan for detail
  • Statistics: avg, min, max
